title: Spike: debugger report-artifact parity vs oracle (lst/bst × .a/.e)
notes: Spike under #13. Built a reusable 2x2 probe (experiments/debugger-report-parity-probe.sh) running oracle×lccjs over run-on-.a/run-on-.e, snapshotting all 8 artifacts + diffing. Finding: NO missing artifact (the remembered symptom did not reproduce, incl. under -d — both write .lst+.bst everywhere; corrected a misread that -d suppresses stats: line364 generateStats=false is under -nostats, not -d). Real diffs are 6 formatting deltas (header A/S padding, stats alignment, src/code column geometry, .a source-line truncation [only content-affecting], BST trailing space, banner date fmt). Reframes #13 to report-format parity; decomposition posted to #13. Ratio 0.38x (5.77m vs C15m) — fast: oracle wired + warm context. In-repo close.

Closes #117.

title: sext semantics research+bug report for Prof Dos Reis (selector=mask, coherent only 2^k-1)
notes: Research ticket split from #144. Validated the user's hypothesis: sext's 2nd operand is a runtime bitmask; coherent sign-extend ONLY for contiguous 2^k-1 selectors, garbage for the other 12/16 low values. Live oracle evidence (sext_field_probe.a r1=0..15 sweep) + lccjs IDENTICAL by diff (it hardcodes SEXT_PARITY_TABLE). actual_min ~11 INCLUDES ~5m pre-worktree survey (reading interpreter.js table/executeSEXT, isa doc, experiments) before the captured start_iso; span alone is ~6m. C set retro (no formal C at grab). Deliverable: docs/research/sext-semantics-report.md. Found 2 side bugs: sext_sweep.a uses out-of-range mov 0x1234; lccjs .string rejects \\n escape. Worktree.

title: Test: lock in .a-path LST source-text parity vs oracle (mnemonics + comments)
notes: Test-only lock-in (behavior already correct per #145). New spec lcc.oracle.source-retention.e2e.spec.js: comments-INCLUDED complement to the existing lcc.oracle.e2e suite (which uses stripComments:true and thus never guards comments). Per demo Q/R/Z: (1) self-contained — every .a source line incl. comments appears in the lccjs .lst; (2) parity vs cached golden with stripComments:false. Reused runJSLCC/compareLstFiles/goldens. Whitespace-lenient (trim+collapse+trunc40) tolerates deviation 11. TDD-verified RED under a comment-stripping mutant (3 self-contained checks fail), GREEN normally. Ratio 0.42x (5.02m vs C12m) — harness already existed. In-repo close.
